SEARCH
Package com.comp2042.controller.game

Class GuiController

java.lang.Object
com.comp2042.controller.game.GuiController
All Implemented Interfaces:
GameInputHandler.InputHandlerCallbacks, javafx.fxml.Initializable
public class GuiController extends Object implements javafx.fxml.Initializable, GameInputHandler.InputHandlerCallbacks
Controller for JavaFX GUI components.

Acts as a coordinator that delegates responsibilities to specialized sub-controllers: GameBoardRenderer, BrickRenderer, AnimationController, GameInputHandler, HudManager, TwoPlayerPanelManager, CountdownManager, and AudioVolumeManager.

Uses Facade and Mediator patterns to coordinate communication between sub-controllers.

Author:
Original code + Dong, Jia

    Description

    • handleKeyPressEvent

      public void handleKeyPressEvent(javafx.scene.input.KeyEvent keyEvent)
      Handles keyboard press events for game controls. Processes movement, rotation, pause, and new game requests. This method is public to allow external components to set up keyboard handlers.
      Parameters:
      keyEvent - The KeyEvent containing information about the key press.

    • initGameView

      public void initGameView(int[][] boardMatrix, ViewData brick)
      Initializes the game view by setting up the visual representation of the board and the initial falling brick based on the provided board matrix and view data.
      Parameters:
      boardMatrix - The initial state of the game board matrix.
      brick - The initial view data for the falling brick (shape, position).

    • refreshGameBackground

      public void refreshGameBackground(int[][] board)
      Updates the visual representation of the static game board background. PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION: Only updates cells that have actually changed.
      Parameters:
      board - The updated board matrix.

    • refreshActiveBrick

      public void refreshActiveBrick(ViewData brick)
      Forces the active brick to refresh its position and shape based on supplied view data. Used when the underlying board resets (e.g., starting a new game) to ensure the brick appears at the intended spawn location before the timeline ticks.
      Parameters:
      brick - the current ViewData for the active brick

    • setEventListener

      public void setEventListener(InputEventListener eventListener)
      Sets the event listener (likely GameController) for handling input events. Also updates the GameInputHandler to ensure consistent event routing.
      Parameters:
      eventListener - The InputEventListener instance.

    • bindScore

      public void bindScore(javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty integerProperty)
      Binds the game score property to a GUI element (e.g., a label). This method is currently a placeholder.
      Parameters:
      integerProperty - The IntegerProperty representing the score.

    • gameOver

      public void gameOver()
      Handles the game over state. Stops the automatic movement timeline and shows the game over panel.

    • rebuildGameForRandomizerChange

      public void rebuildGameForRandomizerChange()
      Rebuilds the underlying game controller and board after a gameplay-affecting settings change (e.g., switching the piece randomizer). Creates a fresh GameController which constructs a new Board based on current settings and re-initializes the view. Any running timers are stopped beforehand.

    • newGame

      public void newGame(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent)
      Handles the request to start a new game. Stops the timeline, hides the game over panel, requests a new game from the event listener, and restarts the timeline.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - The ActionEvent triggering the new game (e.g., from a button).

    • pauseGame

      public void pauseGame(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent)
      Handles the request to pause or unpause the game via a UI button. Delegates the pause request to the GameController or TwoPlayerGameController.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - The ActionEvent triggering the pause/unpause.

    • setPauseStateDirectly

      public void setPauseStateDirectly(boolean paused)
      Sets the pause state directly without triggering pause logic. Used when restoring countdown state.
      Parameters:
      paused - whether the game should be paused

    • resumeGame

      public void resumeGame()
      Resumes the game by restarting the timeline if it exists. This method is called when returning from settings to ensure the game continues.

    • resumeFromOverlay

      public void resumeFromOverlay()
      Resumes gameplay after returning from modal overlays (Settings/Help). Ensures the state machine toggles back to Playing before timers resume.

    • updateProgress

      public void updateProgress(int linesClearedInLevel, int targetLines)

    • updateStarDisplay

      public void updateStarDisplay(int stars)

    • updateLevelSpeedDisplay

      public void updateLevelSpeedDisplay(int levelId)

    • updateBestStats

      public void updateBestStats(int bestScore, long bestTimeMillis)

    • setGameTitleForLevel

      public void setGameTitleForLevel(int levelId)
      Updates the central game title to indicate the active level.
      Parameters:
      levelId - the numeric level identifier to display

    • resetGameTitle

      public void resetGameTitle()
      Resets the central game title to the default label for non-level modes.

    • applyThemeToPreviewDisplays

      public void applyThemeToPreviewDisplays(String accentColor)
      Applies theme colors to Hold and Next preview displays.
      Parameters:
      accentColor - the theme's accent color (e.g., "#FFD700" for gold)

    • showLevelModeUI

      public void showLevelModeUI()

    • isLevelMode

      public boolean isLevelMode()
      Checks if the game is in Level Mode.
      Returns:
      true if in Level Mode, false otherwise

    • hideLevelModeUI

      public void hideLevelModeUI()

    • updateTime

      public void updateTime(int timeLimitSeconds)

    • setLevelModeBoard

      public void setLevelModeBoard(Board board)
      Sets the board reference for Level Mode. This is needed to show the game over screen when time runs out.
      Parameters:
      board - the board instance for Level Mode

    • getCurrentLevel

      public int getCurrentLevel()
      Gets current level for Endless Mode.
      Returns:
      current level

    • updateScore

      public void updateScore(int currentScore, int highScore)
      Updates the score display for EndlessMode.
      Parameters:
      currentScore - the current score
      highScore - the high score

    • updateLines

      public void updateLines(int linesCleared)
      Updates the lines cleared display for EndlessMode.
      Parameters:
      linesCleared - the number of lines cleared

    • updateLevel

      public void updateLevel(int level)
      Updates the level display for EndlessMode.
      Parameters:
      level - the current level

    • updateSpeed

      public void updateSpeed(int speedLevel)
      Updates the speed display for EndlessMode.
      Parameters:
      speedLevel - the current speed level

    • updateGameSpeed

      public void updateGameSpeed(int newSpeed)
      Updates the game speed for EndlessMode.
      Parameters:
      newSpeed - the new drop speed in milliseconds

    • setGhostEnabled

      public void setGhostEnabled(boolean enabled)
      Sets whether ghost piece should be displayed.
      Parameters:
      enabled - true to show ghost piece, false to hide it

    • showLevelUpNotification

      public void showLevelUpNotification(int newLevel)
      Shows a level up notification.
      Parameters:
      newLevel - the new level number

    • updateLevelDisplay

      public void updateLevelDisplay(int level)
      Updates the level display.
      Parameters:
      level - the current level

    • updateNextDisplay

      public void updateNextDisplay(int[][] nextBrickData)
      Updates the next piece display for EndlessMode.
      Parameters:
      nextBrickData - the next brick shape data

    • updateHoldDisplay

      public void updateHoldDisplay(int[][] holdBrickData)
      Updates the hold piece display for EndlessMode.
      Parameters:
      holdBrickData - the held brick shape data

    • toggleMute

      public void toggleMute()
      Toggles mute state for audio. When muted, sets Master Volume to 0%. When unmuted, restores previous volume. This affects all audio (both music and sound effects) through the master volume control.

    • setGameMode

      public void setGameMode(boolean isTwoPlayer)
      Sets the game mode for keyboard binding purposes.
      Parameters:
      isTwoPlayer - true for two-player mode, false for single-player mode

    • updateMuteButtonState

      public void updateMuteButtonState()
      Updates the Mute button state based on current Master Volume. Called when settings are saved from the settings dialog.

    • showSettings

      public void showSettings()
      Shows settings dialog. Opens the settings page with ability to return to current game.

    • showHelp

      public void showHelp()
      Shows help dialog with game mode descriptions. Uses MainMenuController.showHelpDialog() to ensure consistent UI with main menu.

    • returnToMenu

      public void returnToMenu()
      Returns to main menu. Properly cleans up all resources (timelines, timers) before returning.

    • returnToLevelSelection

      public void returnToLevelSelection()
      Returns to the level selection screen when in Level Mode.

    • isEndlessMode

      public boolean isEndlessMode()
      Checks if the current game is in Endless Mode.
      Returns:
      true if in Endless Mode, false otherwise

    • isTwoPlayerMode

      public boolean isTwoPlayerMode()
      Checks if the game is in two-player mode.
      Returns:
      true if in two-player mode, false otherwise

    • setEndlessMode

      public void setEndlessMode(boolean endlessMode)
      Sets the current game mode.
      Parameters:
      endlessMode - true for Endless Mode, false for other modes

    • showEndlessGameOverScene

      public void showEndlessGameOverScene(Board board)
      This method loads the endless game over FXML and switches to it.
      Parameters:
      board - the game board containing final game data

    • showLevelGameOverScene

      public void showLevelGameOverScene(Board board, boolean[] newRecords)
      Shows the Level Mode Game Over scene. Called when a level is completed or failed.
      Parameters:
      board - the game board instance
      newRecords - array containing [isNewBestScore, isNewBestTime], or null if not available

    • showLevelGameOverScene

      public void showLevelGameOverScene(Board board, boolean[] newRecords, int overrideLinesCleared)

    • showLevelGameOverScene

      public void showLevelGameOverScene(Board board, boolean[] newRecords, int overrideLinesCleared, boolean fromLevelTimeout)

    • initPlayer1View

      public void initPlayer1View(int[][] boardMatrix, ViewData brick)
      Initializes Player 1's game view for two-player mode. Sets up the visual representation of Player 1's board and falling brick.
      Parameters:
      boardMatrix - the initial state of Player 1's game board matrix
      brick - the initial view data for Player 1's falling brick

    • initPlayer2View

      public void initPlayer2View(int[][] boardMatrix, ViewData brick)
      Initializes Player 2's game view for two-player mode. Sets up the visual representation of Player 2's board and falling brick.
      Parameters:
      boardMatrix - the initial state of Player 2's game board matrix
      brick - the initial view data for Player 2's falling brick

    • refreshPlayer1Brick

      public void refreshPlayer1Brick(ViewData brick)
      Refreshes Player 1's brick display based on updated view data. Updates the position and shape of Player 1's falling brick.
      Parameters:
      brick - the updated view data for Player 1's brick

    • refreshPlayer2Brick

      public void refreshPlayer2Brick(ViewData brick)
      Refreshes Player 2's brick display based on updated view data. Updates the position and shape of Player 2's falling brick.
      Parameters:
      brick - the updated view data for Player 2's brick

    • handlePlayer1DownEvent

      public void handlePlayer1DownEvent(DownData downData)
      Handles Player 1's down event (brick landing). Updates the board display and handles row clearing animations.
      Parameters:
      downData - the down event data containing row clearing information

    • handlePlayer2DownEvent

      public void handlePlayer2DownEvent(DownData downData)
      Handles Player 2's down event (brick landing). Updates the board display and handles row clearing animations.
      Parameters:
      downData - the down event data containing row clearing information

    • refreshGameBackground1

      public void refreshGameBackground1(int[][] board)
      Refreshes Player 1's game board background display. PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION: Only updates cells that have actually changed.
      Parameters:
      board - the updated board matrix

    • refreshGameBackground2

      public void refreshGameBackground2(int[][] board)
      Refreshes Player 2's game board background display. PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION: Only updates cells that have actually changed.
      Parameters:
      board - the updated board matrix

    • updatePlayer1NextDisplay

      public void updatePlayer1NextDisplay(int[][] nextBrickData)
      Updates Player 1's next piece display.
      Parameters:
      nextBrickData - the next brick shape data

    • updatePlayer2NextDisplay

      public void updatePlayer2NextDisplay(int[][] nextBrickData)
      Updates Player 2's next piece display.
      Parameters:
      nextBrickData - the next brick shape data

    • updatePlayer1HoldDisplay

      public void updatePlayer1HoldDisplay(int[][] holdBrickData)
      Updates Player 1's hold piece display.
      Parameters:
      holdBrickData - the held brick shape data

    • updatePlayer2HoldDisplay

      public void updatePlayer2HoldDisplay(int[][] holdBrickData)
      Updates Player 2's hold piece display.
      Parameters:
      holdBrickData - the held brick shape data

    • updatePlayer1Score

      public void updatePlayer1Score(int score)
      Updates Player 1's score display.
      Parameters:
      score - the new score value

    • updatePlayer2Score

      public void updatePlayer2Score(int score)
      Updates Player 2's score display.
      Parameters:
      score - the new score value

    • updatePlayerStats

      public void updatePlayerStats(int player, PlayerStats stats)
      Updates player statistics display for two-player mode.
      Parameters:
      player - the player number (1 or 2)
      stats - the PlayerStats instance containing the statistics

    • showTwoPlayerGameOver

      public void showTwoPlayerGameOver(int winner, int player1Score, int player2Score)
      Shows the game over screen for two-player mode. Displays the winner, detailed statistics, and action buttons.
      Parameters:
      winner - the winner (1 for Player 1, 2 for Player 2, 0 for tie)
      player1Score - Player 1's final score
      player2Score - Player 2's final score

    • getRootPane

      public javafx.scene.layout.BorderPane getRootPane()
      Gets the root pane for keyboard focus management.
      Returns:
      the root BorderPane

    • showCountdown

      public void showCountdown(Runnable onComplete)
      Shows a countdown (3-2-1) before starting the game. Displays countdown on each player's game panel.
      Parameters:
      onComplete - callback to execute when countdown completes

    • showAttackAnimation

      public void showAttackAnimation(int player, int attackPower)
      Shows an enhanced attack animation on the specified player's board. Includes shockwave, screen shake, and flash effects.
      Parameters:
      player - the player number (1 or 2)
      attackPower - the number of lines being attacked

    • showComboBonus

      public void showComboBonus(int player, int combo, int linesEliminated)
      Shows a combo bonus message.
      Parameters:
      player - the player number (1 or 2)
      combo - the combo count
      linesEliminated - the number of lines eliminated
